hypothesis: A paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies will detect C-reactive protein in whole blood at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching laboratory ELISA sensitivity without requiring sample preprocessing.
objective: Fabricate and validate a paper-based electrochemical immunosensor for CRP detection in unprocessed whole blood at ≤0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, benchmarked against reference ELISA.

references.citation: Bryan T, Luo X, Bueno PR, Davis JJ. An optimised electrochemical biosensor for the label-free detection of C-reactive protein in blood. Biosensors and Bioelectronics. 2013;39(1):94–98. PMID: 22809521

Sun S, Luo J, Zhu Y, Kong F et al. Multifunctional self-driven origami paper-based integrated microfluidic chip to detect CRP and PAB in whole blood. Biosensors and Bioelectronics. 2022;208:114225. PMID: 35358776
Thangamuthu M, Santschi C, Martin OJF. Label-Free Electrochemical Immunoassay for C-Reactive Protein. Biosensors (Basel). 2018;8(2):34.

experiments.name: Sensor Fabrication & Antibody Functionalization
experiments.goal: Fabricate paper-based 3-electrode sensors with AuNP modification and immobilize anti-CRP antibody via EDC/NHS coupling.
experiments.success_criteria: Positive control (5 mg/L CRP) shows ≥40% DPV peak suppression vs blank. Electrode resistance <50 Ω. AuNP UV-Vis λmax 520 ± 5 nm.
steps: Cut Whatman Grade 1 paper into 10×30 mm strips. Define hydrophobic barriers by wax printing (Xerox ColorQube). Bake at 120°C for 2 min.
Screen-print carbon ink on working and counter zones. Print Ag/AgCl ink on reference zone. Cure at 120°C for 30 min. Verify resistance <50 Ω by multimeter.
Synthesise citrate-capped AuNPs (13 nm, Turkevich method). Confirm by UV-Vis λmax 520 nm. Electrodeposit onto working electrode at −0.2 V for 200 s in 0.5 M H2SO4.
Verify AuNP deposition by CV in 5 mM K3[Fe(CN)6]: peak current must increase ≥2× vs bare carbon electrode.
Form MUA SAM: incubate electrode in 10 mM MUA in ethanol overnight at RT. Rinse 3× ethanol, 3× PBS.
Activate with 50 mM EDC + 100 mM NHS in MES buffer pH 6.0 for 30 min. Drop-cast anti-CRP antibody (100 µg/mL, 5 µL). Incubate 2h RT in humidity chamber.
Quench with 1 M ethanolamine pH 8.5, 30 min. Block with 5% BSA in PBS, 1h RT. Store at 4°C (stable ≤2 weeks).
Positive control check: apply 5 mg/L CRP in PBS; DPV peak suppression must be ≥40% vs blank before batch is released.

experiments.name: Matrix Interference Screen
experiments.goal: Select optimal upstream filtration strategy to suppress haemoglobin electrochemical interference while passing CRP analyte to the working electrode.
experiments.success_criteria: Selected strategy: RBC retention >95% (filtrate haematocrit <5%) AND S/N >3 at 0.5 mg/L CRP spike. Gate 1 — block execution if no strategy passes.
steps: Obtain CRP-negative K2EDTA whole blood from IRB-approved donor. Confirm CRP <0.5 mg/L by ELISA.
Spike three aliquots to 0.5 mg/L recombinant CRP.
Test strategy A: nitrocellulose membrane HF135 upstream. Apply 15 µL spiked blood, run DPV at 10 min.
Test strategy B: cellulose acetate 0.45 µm membrane. Same procedure.
Test strategy C: 0.1% saponin lysis layer in upstream paper zone. Same procedure.
Measure haemoglobin in filtrate by UV-Vis at 414 nm. Target <5% of input Hb.
Calculate S/N ratio per strategy. Select highest S/N + lowest Hb passage. Document Gate 1 decision.

experiments.name: DPV Optimisation & Buffer Calibration
experiments.goal: Optimise differential pulse voltammetry parameters for speed and sensitivity. Establish LOD in PBS buffer before advancing to whole blood.
experiments.success_criteria: LOD ≤0.5 mg/L in PBS. Total assay time ≤10 min. Calibration curve R² >0.99 across 0.05–10 mg/L. Gate 2 — proceed to whole blood only if both criteria met.
steps: Connect sensor to portable potentiostat. DPV: step potential 5 mV, amplitude 25 mV, pulse width 50 ms, scan range −0.2 to +0.6 V vs Ag/AgCl. Redox probe: 5 mM K3[Fe(CN)6]/K4[Fe(CN)6] (1:1) in PBS.
Optimise equilibration time: apply 0.5 mg/L CRP (10 µL) and measure DPV at 3, 5, 8, 10 min. Select shortest time giving plateau signal ≤10 min.
Prepare CRP calibration standards in PBS: 0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 5, 10 mg/L. Apply 10 µL per sensor, n=4 per concentration.
Run n=10 blank replicates. Calculate LOD = mean blank + 3σ.
Fit with 4-parameter logistic model. Report R², LOD, dynamic range.
Confirm total assay time ≤10 min before proceeding to whole blood.

experiments.name: Whole Blood Validation & ELISA Benchmarking
experiments.goal: Validate CRP detection in unprocessed whole blood across clinically relevant concentrations. Benchmark against reference ELISA.
experiments.success_criteria: LOD ≤0.5 mg/L in whole blood. Time ≤10 min. Recovery 85–115%. Intra-assay CV <15%. Bland-Altman mean bias <±0.1 mg/L vs ELISA in 0.1–2 mg/L range.
steps: Spike CRP-negative whole blood to 0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0 mg/L recombinant CRP.
Apply 15 µL to biosensor inlet zone with selected filtration membrane. Run DPV at optimised time. n=8 per concentration.
Run scrambled-antibody control electrode in parallel. Non-specific signal must be <10% of CRP-specific signal.
Calculate LOD (mean blank + 3σ), recovery %, and intra-assay CV.
ELISA benchmark: centrifuge 100 µL aliquot at 2000 × g for 10 min. Run CRP ELISA kit on plasma per kit protocol.
Bland-Altman analysis: mean bias and 95% LoA. Passing-Bablok regression: slope (target 0.9–1.1), intercept (target <±0.05 mg/L).

experiments.name: Selectivity Panel & Shelf-Life Assessment
experiments.goal: Confirm sensor specificity against physiological whole-blood interferents and assess storage stability at 4°C and 25°C.
experiments.success_criteria: Signal deviation <±10% vs CRP-alone control for all six interferents. >85% performance retained after 2 weeks at 4°C.
steps: Prepare interferents at physiological concentrations: albumin 40 g/L, haemoglobin 1.5 g/L, IL-6 100 pg/mL, fibrinogen 3 g/L, ascorbic acid 100 µM, uric acid 500 µM.
Spike each into CRP-negative whole blood + 0.5 mg/L CRP. Run DPV. n=4 per interferent.
Calculate % signal deviation from CRP-alone control. Flag any >±10% for engineering follow-up.
Shelf-life: store sensors at 4°C and 25°C. Measure DPV response to 0.5 mg/L CRP at Day 0, 7, 14, 28.
Report T90 (time to 90% initial performance) per storage condition.

materials.name: Interferent reagent panel (albumin, fibrinogen, ascorbic acid, uric acid)

fixed.item: Paper substrate + electrode inks (carbon, Ag/AgCl, 100-strip batch)
AuNP synthesis reagents (HAuCl4, sodium citrate, H2SO4)
SAM + coupling reagents (MUA, EDC, NHS, ethanolamine)
